title: A bill to designate the mountain at the Devils Tower National Monument, Wyoming, as Devils Tower, and for other purposes.
plain_summary: This bill gives an official name to a famous mountain in Wyoming. The mountain is located at Devils Tower National Monument and will now be called Devils Tower. The government must use this name in all of its papers and records when talking about the mountain and the area around it. This makes Devils Tower the official name that everyone in the government has to use.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources.

sections.title: Section 1 Designation of Devils Tower
sections.text: This section makes the mountain at Devils Tower National Monument in Wyoming officially called Devils Tower. The mountain is located at a specific spot that can be found using coordinates forty-four degrees thirty-five minutes twenty-six seconds north and one hundred four degrees forty-two minutes fifty-five seconds west. There is also an area nearby at coordinates forty-four degrees thirty-five minutes twenty-one seconds north and one hundred four degrees forty-one minutes forty-eight seconds west that will also be called Devils Tower. From now on, whenever any United States law, map, rule, order, document, paper, or other government record mentions this mountain and area, it must refer to it as Devils Tower.
